event: Head study of Saint John the Evangelist
symbols: youthful features — John as the youngest of the apostles
profile gaze — attentiveness and spiritual vision
red curling hair — vitality and the fire of love
bare shoulder — vulnerability and intimacy with Christ
description: A close-up head study showing a young person in profile, turned to the right. The figure has abundant reddish-brown curly hair falling to the shoulders, delicate features, and a bare shoulder visible. One hand reaches toward the opposite shoulder. The background is dark, concentrating attention on the luminous skin and rich hair. The expression is gentle and contemplative, with eyes directed to the right as if gazing at something beyond the frame.
theological_description: John the Evangelist, traditionally identified as the 'beloved disciple' who leaned on Jesus's breast at the Last Supper (John 13:23), holds a unique place among the apostles as the one closest to Christ's heart. His youth and beauty in artistic tradition express the spiritual reality of one whose intimacy with the Lord gave him unparalleled insight into divine love. It was John who recorded Jesus's most profound declarations of love — 'God is love' (1 John 4:8) and 'Greater love has no one than this: to lay down one's life for one's friends' (John 15:13). He was the only apostle present at the cross, where Jesus entrusted His mother to John's care (John 19:26-27), and the first to believe at the empty tomb when he 'saw and believed' (John 20:8). This study captures the contemplative quality of one who would later write: 'That which was from the beginning, which we have heard, which we have seen with our eyes, which we have looked at and our hands have touched — this we proclaim concerning the Word of life' (1 John 1:1).

scripture_refs.reason: John as the beloved disciple leaning on Jesus's breast — his defining identity
Jesus entrusting Mary to John at the cross — his unique intimacy with Christ
John's great theological declaration that God is love — the fruit of his contemplative intimacy
John seeing and believing at the empty tomb — the beloved disciple's perception of faith
